name: AU Student Government
summary: AUSG is the preeminent student organization at American University dedicated to enhancing the student experience by representing and advocating for their interests and creating quality programming.

name: AU Club Council
summary: AUCC provides funding to recognized undergraduate clubs on campus, resources needed for clubs to engage the student body, and organizational and leadership development for clubs to be successful on campus.

name: Student Media Board
summary: Student Media Board is the governing board for the 10 student media organizations at American University.

name: Kennedy Political Union
summary: The Kennedy Political Union is the non-partisan central political programming board at AU.

name: Student Union Board
summary: The Student Union Board is the entertainment bureau on campus that strives to bring artists, actors, and other creatives by curating events that are pursuant to the values of the AU community.

name: American Literary Magazine
summary: American Literary Magazine, or AmLit, is American University's student-run literary and visual arts magazine.

name: American Way of Life
summary: AWOL is a student-run magazine and online news source dedicated to subversive, investigative reporting.

name: The Blackprint
summary: The Blackprint is an online and print publication at American University run by students of color and focused on community issues.

name: The Aviary
summary: The Aviary serves as a journalism-focused safe space for women and non-binary AU students and welcomes AU students of all backgrounds to its community.

name: WVAU
summary: American University's student-run, internet-only radio station

name: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People, American University Chapter
summary: To advance the economic, education, social, and political status of our members and their allies.

name: South Asian Student Association
summary: The purpose of the South Asian Student Association is to extend and promote South Asian culture at American University.

name: Caribbean Circle
summary: Caribbean Circle is a cultural organization committed to presenting issues from a Caribbean perspective. It is our goal to create a strong community for students, while showcasing the beauty of our beloved region.

name: Brazilian Student Association at American University (BRASA AU)
summary: Brazilian Student Association at American University (BRASA AU) is dedicated to the promotion and appreciation of Brazilian culture in all of its forms within the American University (AU) community.

name: Black Student Union
summary: We are an organization comprised of and dedicated to serving Black students at American University. The purpose of our organization is to support the social and political aspects of all Black identities through advocacy and programming.

name: Graduate Leadership Council at American University
summary: The representative body of graduate students at American University.

name: Phi Sigma Kappa
summary: Phi Sigma Kappa, Epsilon Triton, is the oldest Fraternity on AU's campus, founded on November 15th, 1936.

name: Sigma Alpha Mu
summary: Hi! We are Sigma Alpha Mu or Sammy for short. Our fraternity is dedicated to fostering lifelong friendships, academic success, and leadership development.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is the Professional Co-ed Business Fraternity open to all students founded in 1904 with the purpose of developing its members into principled business leaders. AKPsi has over 298,000 initiated members at 219 universities in 4 countries

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society (AHS) is a non-partisan, non-profit organization that prepares the next generation of leaders for careers in foreign policy and national security, guided by the Hamiltonian vision of strong, principled American leadership.

name: Alpha Phi Omega National Service Fraternity
summary: Alpha Phi Omega - Eta Phi is American University's professional community service fraternity. We are committed to lifelong duty of service through our cardinal principles of leadership, friendship, and service.

name: Beekeeping Society at American University
summary: Beekeeping Society is open to all students, committed to ethical and sustainable beekeeping on American University’s campus, and dedicated to educating AU about the importance of pollinators and sustainability.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to serve all students and scholars at American University, with a special focus on supporting the Chinese student community and promoting Chinese culture, while welcoming anyone who is interested in our mission,

name: College Republicans at American University
summary: The American University College Republicans aim to promote conservatism on campus and to help elect Republican candidates to public office.

name: Mock Trial @ AU
summary: Mock Trial Team @ AU

name: The Anime Society
summary: The Anime Society is a community (open to all students) that enjoys anime and related media.

name: Association for Computing Machinery
summary: The purpose of American University Chapter of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) is to engage Computer Science students in technology related activities and enrich their knowledge through field trips and speaker sessions.

name: College Democrats @AU
summary: The College Democrats at American University are dedicated to advancing the goals and ideals of the Democratic Party. We aim to mobilize and empower young people to use their political power. College Democrats @AU is open to all students.

name: Gamers at AU
summary: Gamers at AU is American University's primary gaming organization. We meet every Saturday at 7 in MGC 305/306/307. Join us for Board Games, Video Games, Dungeons and Dragons, and other activities!

name: PRIDE at AU
summary: Welcome to PRIDE at AU! We are AU's premiere LGBTQ+ student group. We strive to defend and protect the rights and interests of our members and the AU LGBTQ+ community as a whole. PRIDE at AU is open to all students.

name: Rude Mechanicals of The American University
summary: Crossing swords, lines, and genders since 2003.

name: Audio Engineering Society
summary: The Audio Engineering Society brings students and professionals together to improve their craft. We have student-run events on and off campus, including guest speakers and more!

name: BRAIN (Bringing Research And Interest in Neuroscience) at American University
summary: The purpose of this organization is to foster interest in Neuroscience in the AU community. We exist as an interdisciplinary club, open to individuals of all majors and degrees.

name: Dime a Dozen
summary: American University's Premier Gender Inclusive A Cappella Group!

name: Kogod Accounting Club at American University
summary: Open to all students, the Kogod Accounting Club at American University aims to develop and support a network of students who share interest in the fields of Accounting and Taxation.

name: Kogod Women in Business (GSC-KSB)
summary: Kogod Women in Business is a graduate student organization that empowers its members & other women through networking & professional growth opportunities. This organization is open to all AU students.

name: On A Sensual Note
summary: AU's premier a cappella group. Open to all students.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: We are AU’s award-winning chapter of the Public Relations Student Society of America. AU PRSSA is committed to facilitating exploration of the communications industry for students of any major through our growing our alumni network and private events.

name: Puerto Rican Student Organization
summary: Our purpose is to engage and empower Puerto Rican, Latinx, and non-Latinx students to raise awareness on Puerto Rican issues. PRSO provides a platform for students to hold the necessary discussions and activities on Puerto Rican and Latinx issues.

name: Southeast Asia Student Network at American University
summary: The Southeast Asia Student Network promotes cultural awareness and diversity of Southeast Asia while providing a platform for academic and professional networking in DC. SEANET is open to all students! @seanet.au

name: Sister Sister
summary: Sister Sister provides a safe space on American University’s campus. Through mentorship, community service, and campus events, we build a supportive community for all students while centering the experiences of women of the African diaspora.

name: Undergraduate Women in Business
summary: Undergraduate Women in Business is an organization dedicated to empowering AU students of all backgrounds through providing professional development opportunities and tools for a successful career. This organization is open to all AU students.

name: Women In Audio
summary: Women in Audio brings awareness to women’s contributions in the audio engineering industry as a whole while also fostering a collaborative and supportive environment for students who support our mission on campus.

name: Women in Science
summary: Women in Science is dedicated to showcasing the many activities (professional, academic, research, service & outreach, and social) that students in science at AU are involved in!

name: School of Communication Graduate Student Council
summary: The American University School of Communication Graduate Student Council is a student government association that represents graduate students in SOC.

name: School of Public Affairs Graduate Student Council
summary: We are a student government organization that represents all SPA graduate students.

name: School of International Service Graduate Student Council
summary: The SIS Graduate Student Council represents the academic and social interests of all SIS graduate students.

name: Kogod Graduate Student Council (Kogod GSC)
summary: The Kogod Graduate Student Council is a student led organization that represents graduate students in the Kogod School of Business at American University

name: Days for Girls at AU
summary:
At Days for Girls at AU, we aim to make a global impact through education, advocacy, and fundraising. We work hard to provide menstrual products for anyone who menstruates at AU, in DC, and globally.

Follow us on Instagram @daysforgirlsau

name: College of Arts and Sciences Graduate Student Council
summary: The College of Arts and Sciences Graduate Student Council represents graduate students in the College of Arts & Sciences.

name: Division of Student Affairs
summary: The Division of Student Affairs is home to student-focused departments that provide key services and support around life at AU.

name: Black Girls Vote: AU
summary: "Our mission is to inspire women of color to use the political process and improve the quality of life for their families and the collective community. "

name: Students for a Just Society
summary: Our mission is to raise awareness and facilitate conversation regarding critical issues in the criminal justice system, grounded in action and dedicated to a fair and equitable justice system at the campus, local, and national level.

name: Graduate Black Student Union
summary: The Graduate Black Student Union - open to all students - serves to facilitate interactions between graduate students to strengthen the community, network, provide support, and improve relations between students and alumni of American University

name: Emerging Arts Leaders Symposium (GSC-CAS)
summary: The Emerging Arts Leaders Symposium plans an annual symposium for young professionals who work in the arts.

name: Spirit and Traditions Board
summary: The Spirit and Traditions Board aims to put on events that unite a diverse student body and find ways to celebrate the many great students that make AU what it is today

name: Kogod Private Equity & Venture Capital Club
summary: We strive to create an environment for students to learn more about venture capital and private equity through bi-weekly meetings which allow us to network and connect in the world of private equity and business education. Open to all students.

name: Mu Beta Psi National Honorary Musical Fraternity (ΜΒΨ)
summary: Mu Beta Psi is a professional service-based music organization. Our members help plan and support music-related events both on and off campus.

name: Albanian Cultural Club
summary: Albanian Cultural Club aims to introduce AU students to the rich aspects of Albania's culture, art, history, and heritage. We aim to contribute to AU's diverse campus by creating cultural communication between our peers.

name: Revolution: The AU Fashion Society
summary: Revolution: The AU Fashion Society is an organization for anyone interested in fashion on AU's campus. We hold events and discussions and create a space for those who enjoy fashion, while exposing students to career realities and the fashion industry.

name: Best Buddies @ AU
summary: The organization is dedicated to ending the social, physical, and economic isolation of people with people of intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD).

name: Center for Leadership & Community Engagement
summary: At the Center for Leadership and Community Engagement – or CLCE – we lead the AU community into the world to serve. From projects around DC to trips abroad, we stand with a university dedicated to improving lives in our global community.

name: School of International Service Undergraduate Council
summary:
The SIS undergraduate council serves as liaison between the SIS students and SIS faculty. Our responsiblility is to advocate issues and establish programming of interest for the SIS student body.

Email sisundergradcouncil@american.edu to get involved!

name: Residence Hall Association (RHA)
summary: The Residence Hall Association serves to support and improve the quality of residence life for the students living in on-campus housing at American University. Here we share all upcoming events presented by the Residence Hall Association.

name: Active Minds at American University
summary: Active Minds at AU works to change the culture surrounding mental health by raising awareness of mental health resources, encouraging positive mental health, raising funds, hosting events that surround mental health and advocate for change on campus.

name: Arabic Club
summary: The Arabic Club at AU aims to introduce non-native Arabic speakers to all things Arabic language and culture. We bring in guest speakers and practice speaking Arabic outside of the classroom. We hope you will join us!

name: EGO
summary: AU's fashion magazine

name: Disabled Student Union at American University
summary: DSU brings together disabled undergraduates and our allies. From social programming to advocacy, we’re building a disability-welcoming community at our organization and across AU.

name: Art Club at American University
summary:
⋆｡°✩Art Club at American University✩°｡⋆
A safe space for the AU community to collaborate, explore, create and express themselves through art 
We host art sessions from 8:00-10:00pm at Katzen 104 every Wednesday! 
No level of expertise is needed :)

name: It's On Us At American University
summary: A community in which students and survivors feel safe from sexual violence on campus.

name: University College
summary: A first year Living-Learning Community.

name: AU Compass
summary: A sophomore Living-Learning Community

name: AU Cornerstone
summary: An invite-only program that allows first-year students to study abroad or complete an internship.

name: Career Center
summary: The American University Career Center serves CAS, SOC, and SPA students and the AU community's career and professional development needs.

name: Latinos En Acción
summary: Established in 2022 to advocate for immigrants' rights and representation while empowering and creating a community on campus. We are a chapter of the United We Dream Action DMV (UWD), a national non-profit organization.

name: Brother Brother AU
summary: A coalition dedicated to building brotherhood through mentorship and community. This organization seeks to connect, educate, and support incoming and existing members of The American University.

name: Marketing Association at American University
summary: We are a community of individuals who are passionate about the marketing field. MAAU is open to students of all majors interested in learning more about marketing.

name: School of Communication Undergraduate Council
summary: The School of Communication Undergraduate Council aims to serve as a resource through diversity and inclusion, programming, social media, and outreach to the SOC community. We will address the concerns and amplify the voices of all SOC students.

name: College of Arts and Sciences Undergraduate Council
summary: College of Arts and Sciences Undergraduate Council

name: School of Education Undergraduate Council
summary: The School of Education Undergraduate Council is a student-run space to build community within the School of Education.

name: Sine Institute
summary: Where the brightest minds engage to promote common ground and nonpartisan solutions.

name: Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages
summary: The AU TESOL program aims to be more than just a graduate program, and believes in the importance of creating a community of learners as well as connecting our students to the expanding ESL community in the Washington, metropolitan area.

name: Center for Language Exploration Acquisition and Research
summary: The Center for Language Exploration Acquisition and Research (CLEAR) offers language learning support services to current American University students.

name: Center for Advocacy and Student Equity
summary: The Center for Advocacy and Student Equity ("C.A.S.E.") is a student-led office that assists the student body get the resources they need on campus.

name: Center for Teaching Research & Learning
summary: The Center for Teaching, Research & Learning (CTRL) promotes excellence in teaching and scholarship at American University. CTRL serves as the nexus for faculty development.

name: Juris Mentem Law Review
summary: Juris Mentem is a student-led research publication at American University, charged with providing students an opportunity to learn and practice the ability to engage in scholarly legal discourse

name: New Student and Family Programs
summary: New Student and Family Programs

name: Entrepreneurship Club at American University
summary: The Entrepreneurship Club at American University is a student-run organization, with the mission to develop leaders who embody the characteristics of being innovative, moral, sustainable, fiscally prudent, and philanthropic.

name: Crochet Club
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to promote a sense of community among crafters and makers.

name: Italian Cultural Society of American University
summary: seeks to promote the culture and language of Italy

name: Students For Reproductive Justice
summary: Students for Reproductive Justice is open to all students. It serves as a safe space for all students to engage in the fight for safe and free reproductive healthcare.

summary: Dedicated to fostering community among all students while promoting cultural awareness, our organization serves as an inclusive space to learn, share, and connect around Indigenous cultures.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary

summary: AU Abroad is the primary office for undergraduate study abroad programs. There are options to study abroad for the semester, year, and summer. We have over 100+ options for any major and minor.

name: First-Generation Graduate Society
summary: Committed to advancing the goals of first generation and low-income graduate students, their welfare, representation, and community longevity at American University.

name: Birders of American University
summary: Birders of AU is a club designed to cultivate or expand upon one's admiration for birds and birdwatching. We do this by inviting guest speakers, watching (un-)educational films about birds, and of course, birdwatching!

name: Pop Culture Club at AU
summary: The Pop Culture Club is dedicated to providing a space where pop culture lovers can gather and share their ideas on society's current events. Catering to all aspects (music, film, celebrities) we aim to foster a strong creative community of AU students.

name: SACNAS Chapter at American University
summary: This organization is open to all students and strives to create an empowering community for students interested in the STEM field at AU. We strive to promote professionalism, research opportunities, networking events, and community involvement.

name: Law Students for Government and Public Policy
summary: LSGP helps students explore careers in government and policy through panels, networking, and conversations with leaders shaping public service and law.

summary: The Business Law Society at AU is a student-run organization centered on promoting the professional and educational excellence of its members in the fields and intersection of business and law. The Business Law Society at AU is open to all students. Amer

name: Japanese Cultural Exchange
summary: Japanese Cultural Exchange is open to all students at American University who are interested in exploring and celebrating Japanese culture! We welcome students from all backgrounds and identities to participate in our programs, events, and community.

name: The Rolling Scones Baking Club
summary: The Rolling Scones Baking Club will build a stress-free environment where members can enjoy baking and eating delicious treats!

name: Fashion and Art Law Society (FALS)
summary: The Fashion and Art Law Society (“FALS”) is dedicated to fostering a community of law students interested in fashion and art law.

name: AU Kitchen
summary: AU Kitchen is the place for all things dining services at American University! Bringing innovation  to your kitchen away from home.

name: Education Law Society
summary: Offer advice and networking for those interested in education law

summary:
The mission of the Art and Cultural Heritage Law Society is to encourage interest in Art and

Cultural Heritage law, foster relationships between the student body and the broader Art and the 

Cultural Heritage community.

name: Hospitality and Tourism Law Program
summary: Build your career path in hotel law

summary: Undergraduate historical review journal that publishes submissions of original academic research from undergraduate students across the country.

name: Fair Housing Law Society
summary: Student organization dedicated to advocacy around fair housing

name: Disabled Grad Student Union at American University (GLC)
summary: DGSUs purpose is to function as an affinity group for Disabled graduate students and students interested in allyship and our mission at American University; the organization creates network of support for students on campus. DGSU is open to all students.

name: Health Law and Policy Student Association
summary: A student-run organization that seeks to promote health law opportunities for AUWCL students. HLPSA seeks to encourage professional development, explore career opportunities, expand the health law curriculum, and raise awareness of the right to health.

name: Family Law Society
summary: Supporting WCL student interest in family law.

name: AUWCL Softball
summary: formation of team(s) for club and intramural play.

name: American Constitution Society
summary: The Washington College of Law chapter of the American Constitution Society supports and advocates for laws and legal systems that redress the founding failures of our Constitution, uphold the rule of law, and realize the promise of equality for all.

name: Caribbean Law Student Association
summary: The Caribbean Law Student Association (CLSA) at Washington College of Law (WCL) is a group that is committed to fostering an inclusive community for individuals of Caribbean descent and those who share a passion for Caribbean culture.

name: Lincoln Scholars Program
summary: Lincoln Scholars (Certificate in Political Thought) is a 15-credit certificate program for 1st and 2nd year students who want to explore the great questions of moral and political life in a context of intellectual and political diversity.

name: Department of Environment, Development and Health (EDH) in SIS
summary: The Dept of Environment, Development & Health (EDH) is home to the EDH House, four graduate programs, two thematic areas, and one innovative cohort, the Earth Scholars. EDH's mission is simple - to defend and care for life on Earth.

name: French Club at AU
summary: The French Club at AU fosters community among students passionate about French language and Francophone culture on campus. French speakers of all levels are welcome! This club is open to all students!

name: Students Against Cancer
summary: We strive to provide support and resources to anyone in the AU community who is affected by cancer, and we want to provide knowledge and advocacy opportunities open to all students.

name: Office of Undergraduate Education
summary: The Office of Undergraduate Education's goal is to help students navigate their undergraduate education at AU.

name: Association for Wargaming
summary:
Through gameplay, discussion, and collaborations with external professional wargaming institutions, we promote the professional practice and hobby of wargaming. 
We also have a GLC component, all students are welcome at our and their events!

name: Strategic Studies Review (SIS GSC)
summary: The SSR is a graduate student–run, peer-reviewed journal at the American University (School of International Service Graduate Student Council). It promotes academic rigor, policy relevance, and diverse perspectives on foreign policy, security, economics.

name: Doctors without Borders at American University
summary: Our chapter supports the mission of Doctors Without Borders/Médecins Sans Frontières by raising awareness of global health issues, educating our campus, and organizing advocacy and fundraising to aid people in crisis worldwide.

name: Collegiate Institute for Middle Eastern Affairs
summary: The Collegiare Institute for Middle Eastern Affairs is American University's a 501c(3) non-profit, think tank, and professional development organization focused on creating future leaders in the realm of Middle Eastern and North African affairs.

name: Program on Gender, Theory, Law & Practice (GTLP)
summary: The Program on Gender, Theory, Law & Practice (GTLP) at American University Washington College of Law advances critical, intersectional approaches to gender and the law. Building on a strong foundation in feminist legal theory, GTLP supports scholarship,

name: United Against Inequities in Disease
summary: The United Against Inequities in Disease (UAID) chapter at American university is a student led organization aiming to understand and eliminate health inequities in the D.C. Metropolitan area through sustainable solutions.
